front: What is the primary goal of CPU scheduling criteria?
back: To develop a scheduling algorithm that optimizes a specific set of desirable system characteristics, such as maximizing CPU utilization or minimizing waiting time.

front: Define CPU Utilization as a scheduling criterion.
back: A metric that aims to keep the CPU as busy as possible. It is the fraction of time the CPU is actively executing processes, and a high percentage is desirable.

The total amount of time taken to execute a particular process, from the time of submission to the time of completion. It is the sum of the periods spent waiting in the ready queue, executing on the CPU, and performing I/O.

front: What is the difference between Waiting Time and Response Time?
back: Waiting Time is the total time a process spends waiting in the ready queue. Response Time is the time from the submission of a request until the first response is produced (especially important in interactive systems).

front: Why is Throughput an important scheduling criterion?
back: It measures the number of processes completed per unit of time. A scheduler should aim to maximize throughput to indicate efficient processing of jobs.
